article_meta.title: ABC NewsLiveVideoShowsShopInterest Successfully AddedWe'll notify you here with news aboutTurn on desktop notifications

reasoning: Multiple attribution rule violations: the model flagged quoted/alleged statements as author bias and treated attributed claims as the author's voice. Most flagged items are not genuine author-prose bias; any potential structural slant was not properly identified. Output also used code fences, violating format.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged direct quotes and clearly attributed claims (Smith’s accusation; Smith’s quoted language; administration’s assertion) as bias, violating the rule to exclude sourced/quoted text.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1-3 are attributed or quoted, not author bias. Item 4 is weak evidence for slant on its own. Overall, most flags are not defensible author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: If any bias exists, it would be structural slant/story emphasis (e.g., headline foregrounding an accusation, imbalance of detail). The model did not cogently identify such structural bias, but clear author-prose bias is limited.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Since the flagged items largely aren’t valid author-bias instances, labels are effectively misapplied. The lone potentially valid slant claim is under-supported.
